name: Defence Research & Development
student_summary: Build India's defence tech — DRDO, defence PSUs and private defence.
overview: Build India's defence tech — DRDO, defence PSUs and private defence. Defence Research & Development sits in the Security Technology family of Defence, Security & Emergency Services. It is a broad career direction — you can enter it through several routes and specialize later without restarting.
who_is_this_for: You keep asking why until you reach the bottom of things
who_should_avoid: You want a short, easy study path — this one demands years of serious preparation
You want a laptop-anywhere lifestyle — this work happens in person
daily_responsibilities: Apply applied r&d to real problems
Collaborate with your team using systems testing
Keep improving at documentation — the field keeps moving
Communicate progress, results and decisions to the people who depend on them
work_environment: Laboratories and research facilities with focused desk analysis
scope: Demand outlook in India is booming; see FAQs for how the platform keeps this current.
advantages: Demand is booming: opportunities across India and beyond
A broad Security Technology foundation that opens multiple specializations later
challenges: A long, demanding preparation and learning curve
Location-bound work; postings and relocation are part of the deal
Early years demand patience before the rewards compound
misconceptions: “You must pick your exact job title now.” Defence Research & Development is a direction — specialization comes later.
“Only toppers make it.” Consistent skill-building beats one exam rank in the long run.

projects: Beginner: explore applied r&d with a small self-chosen project

faqs: What does Defence Research & Development pay in India?
Entry roles typically start around ₹7 LPA and senior professionals reach ₹20 LPA or more. Live, region-specific salary data is retrieved by the platform when you ask.
What should I study after Class 12?
Focus on Physics, Mathematics. Typical degrees: B.Tech/M.Sc + DRDO SET/GATE, PhD for scientist roles. Alternatives exist: agniveer entry + service ladder.
Will AI take over this career?
AI impact is minimal — deeply human/physical work. Broad paths like Defence Research & Development evolve rather than disappear — the students who pair domain skill with AI tools benefit most.
Is this a broad path or a specific job?
Defence Research & Development is a career direction. Once inside it you can specialize further — the platform will map specializations in future versions.
